data.title: REG - Elementis PLC - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 2576I Elementis PLC 19 November 2025  

19 November 2025

Elementis plc

("Elementis" or the "Company")

Transaction in Own Shares

Elementis announces that, in accordance with the terms of its share buyback programme announced on 28 May 2025 (the "Share Buyback Programme"), the Company purchased the following number of its own ordinary shares of 5p each through Numis Securities Limited ("Deutsche Numis") for cancellation at an average price of 155.6041 pence per share:

Date of purchase:

19 November 2025

Aggregate number of ordinary shares purchased:

140,000

Lowest price paid per share (GBp):

154.60

Highest price paid per share (GBp):

156.40

Volume weighted average price paid per share (GBp):

155.6041

Following settlement of the above purchases and cancellation of the purchased ordinary shares, the Company's total number of ordinary shares in issue shall be 571,526,515. The Company holds 133,329 ordinary shares in treasury and therefore the total number of voting rights in the Company will be 571,526,515. This figure for the total number of voting rights may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

data.title: Paramount Has Spoken to Middle-East Funds About Investing in Warner Deal, Sources Say — WSJ
data.description:
By Joe Flint and Lauren Thomas

Paramount has had early discussions with Middle Eastern sovereign-wealth funds about investing in its potential tie-up with Warner Bros. Discovery if and when such a deal closes, according to people familiar with the matter.

The talks with Saudi Arabia's sovereign-wealth fund and other big Gulf investors are ongoing, and the funds aren't expected to be named in the nonbinding proposal that Paramount will submit this week as suitors bid on Warner Discovery, the people said.

Paramount's bid is expected to be in the same range of its latest unsolicited proposal, which valued Warner Discovery at $23.50 a share, some of the people said.

Any foreign investors would likely only play a role, if at all, should Paramount secure a deal for Warner Discovery and decide to bring in outside investors after the transaction closes, the people said.

The Financial Times earlier reported that Paramount CEO David Ellison had recently met with Saudi's Public Investment Fund and other officials from the region.

Paramount's majority-cash bid is fully backed by the Ellison family, which includes Chief Executive David Ellison's billionaire father, Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison, and private-equity firm RedBird Capital Partners.

The deadline for initial bids for Warner Discovery is Thursday. Besides Paramount, cable and entertainment company Comcast and streamer Netflix are among potential bidders, The Wall Street Journal has reported. Warner Discovery would like to conclude the auction by the end of the year.

Paramount is expected to bid for all of Warner Discovery, including its movie and television studios, the HBO Max streaming service and a portfolio of cable networks that includes CNN, TNT and Food Network. It has already made multiple bids that Warner Discovery has rebuffed.

Comcast and Netflix are interested in Warner Discovery's movie and television studios, film and TV library and HBO Max.

The younger Ellison on Tuesday attended a dinner hosted by the White House to coincide with the visit to Washington by Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman (also known as MBS). Also at the gathering were Apple CEO Tim Cook and Tesla CEO Elon Musk, among other tech-industry leaders.

Dealings with Saudi Arabia have been a touchy subject in Hollywood since the 2018 murder of Washington Post journalist Jamal Khashoggi. The Central Intelligence Agency at the time assessed that MBS orchestrated the killing. President Trump on Tuesday said that the Saudi royal "knew nothing about" the murder.

Write to Joe Flint at Joe.Flint@wsj.com and Lauren Thomas at lauren.thomas@wsj.com

data.title: SOI: H1'26 revenue dropped 32% year-over-year, but EBITDA margin rose to 34.1%
data.description:
Revenue fell 32% year-over-year in H1'26, with strong Edge & Cloud AI offset by weak Mobile and Automotive. EBITDA margin improved to 34.1%, but net loss reached €67m due to one-off impairment and FX losses. Q3'26 revenue is expected to grow sequentially.

Original document: Soitec SA [SOI] Earnings Release — Nov. 20 2025

Disclaimer
This is an AI-generated summary and may contain inaccuracies. Please verify any important information with the original source.

data.title: Fitch Assigns 'BBB-' Rating to JBS N.V.'s IDRs; Outlook Stable
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-São Paulo-19 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has assigned a 'BBB-' Long-Term Issuer Default Rating (IDR) to JBS N.V. (JBS NV) and to the notes co-issued by JBS NV with JBS USA Food Company and JBS USA Foods Group Holdings, Inc.

Fitch currently rates JBS S.A.'s (JBS SA) Long-Term Foreign and Local Currency Issuer Default Ratings (IDRs) at 'BBB-' with a Stable Rating Outlook and its National Scale rating at 'AAA(bra)'/Stable and JBS USA Holding Lux S.a.r.l.'s (JBS USA) Long-Term IDR 'BBB-'/Stable. Fitch also rates JBS USA's short-term rating at 'F3'.

After the corporate reorganization of JBS Group with the listing on the NYSE, JBS NV became the consolidated entity of the group. JBS's ratings reflect its strong business profile, favorable debt amortization, and expected positive FCF and gradual leverage reduction over the next two years.

Key Rating Drivers

Solid Business Profile: JBS's business profile is strong due to its size and diversification across geographies and protein types including pork, poultry, beef, salmon, lamb and prepared foods. It is one of the most geographically diversified companies in the protein sector rated by Fitch, with a substantial presence in North America, South America, Australia and Europe.

Improved Cash Flow Generation: Fitch expects JBS to generate USD5.9 billion of EBITDA in 2025 and USD6.3 billion in 2026, with margins close to 6.9% and 7.3%, respectively, compared with USD6.1 billion EBITDA and EBITDA margins of 7.9% in 2024. Fitch projects that the strong results across most of its business divisions should be maintained, especially for Seara and Pilgrim's Pride. FCF should be negative in 2025 and USD744 million positive in 2026, considering annual investments of USD2.0 billion in 2025 and 2026, and dividends around USD2 billion in 2025 and USD1 billion in 2026.

Net Leverage Below 2.5x: Fitch expects JBS's net leverage to be close to 2.5x by YE 2025 and 2.2x by YE 2026. This reflects stronger performance across various divisions. Specifically, Seara and the U.S. poultry and pork segment are likely to have improved operational margins due to lower grain prices and more balanced supply-demand dynamics. However, the profitability of the U.S. beef division may continue to be affected by cyclical downturns.

Neutral Global Protein Outlook: The global protein's neutral sector outlook reflects steady global consumption led by chicken as consumers shift demand toward lower-priced chicken and pork from higher-priced beef. The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) forecasts global meat consumption to rise 0.2% in 2025, driven by chicken and partly offset by modest declines in beef and pork.

ESG - Governance: J&F owns about 48% of JBS's shares. The Batista family continues to play an active role in managing the group at both board and operational levels, as well as in strategic decision-making. JBS recently concluded its dual listing on the NYSE in the U.S. and B3 in Brazil via Brazilian Depositary Receipts. Fitch regards the dual listing as positive from a credit standpoint, as it would broaden JBS's investor base and potentially enhance the company's transparency and financial flexibility, including the use of equity as a funding source.

Peer Analysis

JBS has a strong business profile as the world's largest beef, poultry, and leather producer, with diversification in pork, salmon, lamb, plant-based protein and prepared food. This geographic diversity enables the company to mitigate business volatility inherent to the industry, placing it in a favorable position from a business risk perspective versus Marfrig Global Foods S.A. (BB+/Stable) and Minerva S.A. (BB/Stable), and in a comparable position to Tyson Foods Inc. (BBB/Stable).

Key Assumptions

--JBS revenue growth of 3.5% in 2025 and 3.4% in 2026;

--Annual investments of USD2.0 billion in 2025 and 2026;

--Dividend payments around USD2.0 billion in 2025 and around 1 billion in 2026.

RATING SENSITIVITIES

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

--Net leverage above 2.5x on a sustained basis in conjunction with negative FCF and weak liquidity;

--Worsening of protein industry fundamentals caused by higher input costs, prolonged protein supply/demand imbalance, structural industry changes that leads to increased operating volatilities, trade policy issues or a loss of market share.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

--Net debt/EBITDA below 1.5x on a sustained basis;

--Improved governance.

Liquidity and Debt Structure

JBS's liquidity is strong due to robust CFO and good access to banks and capital markets debt. Consolidated cash and cash equivalents were about USD3.6 billion and total debt was USD20.8 billion as of Sept. 30, 2025, of which USD999 million is due in the short term. In addition, JBS USA has USD2.9 billion in fully committed available credit lines and USD1 billion in a commercial paper program, and JBS Brazil has USD500 million in an RCF.

Issuer Profile

JBS N.V. is the largest global beef and poultry producer, and the second-largest pork producer in the world.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. MACROECONOMIC ASSUMPTIONS AND SECTOR FORECASTS Click here to access Fitch's latest quarterly Global Corporates Sector Forecasts Monitor data file which aggregates key data points used in our credit analysis. Fitch's macroeconomic forecasts, commodity price assumptions, default rate forecasts, sector key performance indicators and sector-level forecasts are among the data items included. ESG Considerations

JBS N.V. has an ESG Relevance Score of '5' for Governance Structure due to ownership concentration, which has a negative impact on the credit profile and is highly relevant to the rating, resulting in a lower rating.

JBS N.V. has an ESG Relevance Score of '4' for Waste & Hazardous Materials Management; Ecological Impacts due to supply chain management and its exposure to cattle sourcing, which has a negative impact on the credit profile and is relevant to the rating in conjunction with other factors.

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

JBS N.V.; Long Term Issuer Default Rating; New Rating; BBB-; Rating Outlook Stable

----senior unsecured; Long Term Rating; New Rating; BBB-

data.title: QUOTEMEDIA INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
QuoteMedia Inc., a leading provider of market data and financial applications, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements, showcasing growth in revenue and strategic business developments.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: QuoteMedia Inc. reported total revenue of $5.15 million for the three-month period ended September 30, 2025, marking a 10% increase compared to the same period in 2024. For the nine-month period, total revenue reached $14.91 million, a 6% rise from the previous year. The growth was driven by higher average revenue per customer and larger customer acquisitions.

Gross Profit: The company's gross profit for the three-month period was $2.50 million, a 15% increase from the same period in 2024, with a gross margin improvement to 48%. For the nine-month period, gross profit was $6.89 million, a 2% increase, despite a decrease in gross margin to 46% due to higher costs of revenue.

Operating Loss: Operating loss improved to $(0.37) million for the three-month period, compared to $(0.41) million in the same period in 2024. However, for the nine-month period, operating loss increased to $(1.54) million from $(0.72) million, primarily due to higher software development expenses.

Net Loss: The net loss for the three-month period was $(0.37) million, an improvement from $(0.44) million in the same period in 2024. For the nine-month period, net loss increased to $(1.72) million from $(0.72) million, impacted by increased operating expenses.

Basic and Diluted Loss Per Share: The basic and diluted loss per share remained unchanged at $(0.00) for the three-month period, while for the nine-month period, it was $(0.02) compared to $(0.00) in the same period in 2024, reflecting the higher net loss.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: QuoteMedia Inc. operates three main product lines: Interactive Content and Data APIs, Data Feed Services, and Portfolio Management Systems. The Portfolio Management Systems segment, which includes Corporate Quotestream and Individual Quotestream, showed significant growth. Corporate Quotestream revenue increased by 18% for the three-month period and 14% for the nine-month period ending September 30, 2025.

Geographical Performance: Approximately 36% of the company's revenue and 39% of its expenses are denominated in Canadian dollars. The depreciation of the Canadian dollar against the U.S. dollar slightly decreased Canadian dollar revenues and expenses when translated into U.S. dollars, but had minimal impact on net income and cash flows due to the balanced revenue and expenses.

New Product Launches: The company continues to develop and launch new modules of QMod, a proprietary web delivery system designed for secure market data provisioning and ease of integration. QMod is a key component of the company's strategy to meet the demand for mobile-ready, SEO-friendly web content.

Future Outlook: QuoteMedia Inc. plans to expand its product lines and improve infrastructure for the remainder of 2025 and into 2026. This includes adding more features and data to existing products, leveraging AI tools for automation, and expanding data and news coverage. The company also aims to increase sales of its Interactive Content and Data APIs and grow its Data Feed Services client base.

Operational Strategy: The company is focusing on marketing Quotestream for deployment by brokerage firms to their retail clients and expanding into the investment professional market with Quotestream Professional. It is also working on new deployments of trade integration capabilities to enhance its Quotestream applications.

Cost Management: QuoteMedia Inc. reduced the number of development staff in late 2024 as major development projects near completion. However, development costs increased due to a higher percentage of development salaries being expensed rather than capitalized, impacting earnings but not cash flow.

Product Development: Important development projects include the expansion of data and news coverage, introduction of new market information products, and creation of proprietary data sets and analytics. These efforts aim to offer new data products and lower the company's product cost structure.

SEC Filing: QUOTEMEDIA INC [ QMCI ] - 10-Q - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: Adapti, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Adapti, Inc., a company transitioning from health and beauty product sales to a technology-driven entity in the sports management and marketing industry, has released its latest Form 10-Q report. The report highlights substantial financial growth and a strategic shift in business focus following the acquisition of Ballengee Group, LLC, a sports management agency.

Financial Highlights

Revenues: Adapti, Inc. reported revenues of $2.13 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, a significant increase from $2,030 in the prior year period. This growth was primarily driven by the acquisition of Ballengee Group.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $0.80 million, compared to $2,030 in the prior year period, indicating improved profitability due to higher revenues.

Loss from Operations: The loss from operations was $(2.41) million, a substantial increase from $(141,315) in the prior year period, primarily due to increased operating expenses.

Net Loss: Adapti, Inc. reported a net loss of $(2.60) million, compared to $(152,042) in the prior year period, reflecting higher interest expenses and operating costs.

Net Loss Per Common Share (Basic and Diluted): The net loss per common share was $(0.28), compared to $(0.10) in the prior year period, due to the increased net loss and a higher number of shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company's subsidiary, Ballengee Group, primarily generates revenue through agent commissions on Major League Baseball (MLB) contracts and marketing income. For the three months ended September 30, 2025, Ballengee's revenue was primarily derived from $2,020,591 in agent commissions on MLB contracts and $102,335 in marketing income.

Business Focus Shift: Adapti, Inc. has shifted its strategic focus from health and beauty product sales to becoming a technology-driven company in the sports management, marketing, and athlete representation industries. This shift was marked by the acquisition of Ballengee Group, LLC, a sports management agency.

Technology Development: The company is developing AdaptAI, a proprietary AI-driven tool designed to optimize alignment between brands and social media influencers. AdaptAI is currently in the beta stage, with plans for completion by December 31, 2026, subject to securing sufficient funding.

Operational Integration: Following the acquisition of Ballengee Group, the company is focused on integrating Ballengee's operations with its own, leveraging Ballengee's existing client base of approximately 200 professional athletes, including over 40 Major League players.

Future Outlook: The company plans to expand AdaptAI's capabilities to support automated content creation and enable end-to-end generative content workflows for social media publishing and campaign execution. This is expected to enhance the production and distribution of digital content across client campaigns.

Discontinuation of Previous Product Lines: The company has ceased efforts to market or sell health and beauty products, including the Dermacia brand, and is in the process of selling off retained inventory. Future revenues are expected to be derived primarily from athlete representation fees, sponsorships, and platform-enabled brand integrations.

SEC Filing: Adapti, Inc. [ ADTI ] - 10-Q - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: Bradford Custom Homes Featured on 'Trending Today' on Fox Business for Its Purpose Driven and Wellness Minded Approach to Luxury Homebuilding
data.description:
ATLANTA, GEORGIA / ACCESS Newswire / November 19, 2025 / Bradford Custom Homes, a leading Southeastern luxury home builder known for its values driven and wellness minded approach to design and construction, will be featured on an upcoming episode of "Trending Today" airing Saturday, November 23 at 5:30 PM ET on Fox Business. The episode offers a behind the scenes look at how Bradford creates legacy grade homes through craftsmanship, intentional planning, and a client first philosophy.

For more than a decade, "Trending Today" has highlighted global innovation across business, luxury lifestyle, design, and entrepreneurship. The series showcases standout brands that are shaping the future of how people live and work. Bradford Custom Homes was selected for its commitment to building with purpose, centering timeless architecture, healthy living principles, integrity, and thoughtful design throughout every project.

"At Bradford Custom Homes, we believe luxury is more than finishes. It is the responsibility to build healthier and higher performing homes that last for generations," said Brad Robinson, President of Bradford Custom Homes. "Our responsibility is to build homes that honor the families who will live in them, today and for generations." Ruskin said it best, ‘When we build, let us think that we build forever.' That belief is woven into every decision we make. Our episode of ‘Trending Today' offers a rare look behind the curtain at how our team blends craftsmanship, intentional design, and genuine client care to create legacy grade homes. We are honored to share our story on a national stage and to highlight the partners and people who make this possible every day."

In the episode, viewers will see how Bradford's core values guide every phase of the building process, from early design and planning to the hands on craftsmanship that brings each home to life. While the feature focuses on architectural beauty and the client experience, these values also reflect Bradford's deeper philosophy of creating homes that support the way high performing families want to live. Bradford builds for people who want their home to perform at the level they aspire to perform. A home is the place where people recharge and rejuvenate, and the right wellness based amenities allow that to happen. Better water, cleaner air, and lighting that supports clarity and circadian balance help create an environment that elevates daily living and long term wellbeing.

The segment also highlights Bradford's elevated client experience, defined by transparency, communication, and a deep sense of partnership throughout the build. This commitment to integrity and wellness minded decision making is at the heart of Bradford's work across its expanding Southeastern presence, including Atlanta, Charleston and Kiawah Island, and the Highlands and Cashiers mountain region.

"For us, the story is always about the people. The families we build for and the team that brings their vision to life," Robinson added.

"Trending Today" continues to spotlight exceptional companies whose work reflects purpose, innovation, and craftsmanship. The feature on Bradford Custom Homes gives national viewers a glimpse into the values and vision that have earned the company its reputation as one of the Southeast's premier luxury builders.

To learn more about "Trending Today," visit www.trendingtoday.com.

About Bradford Custom Homes

Bradford Custom Homes is a luxury residential builder creating legacy grade and wellness minded homes across the Southeastern United States. The company builds for people who want their home to perform at the level they aspire to perform. A Bradford home is designed to be a place where families can recharge and rejuvenate through healthier water, cleaner air, and lighting that supports clarity and circadian balance. Known for timeless architecture, intentional design, and an elevated client experience, Bradford blends craftsmanship with purpose driven and health conscious values to build long lasting homes that honor the families who live in them. With active projects across Atlanta, Charleston and Kiawah Island, and the Highlands and Cashiers region, Bradford partners with leading architects, designers, and artisans to deliver homes rooted in beauty, integrity, and longevity.

About Trending Today

"Trending Today," the acclaimed television series airing on Fox Business, A and E, and Bloomberg, showcases groundbreaking ideas, visionary brands, and innovative leaders across luxury living, technology, wellness, design, and entrepreneurship. Each episode features carefully selected companies that are shaping the future through craftsmanship, creativity, and purpose driven work. To learn more, visit www.trendingtoday.com.

Media Contact

Liz Plummer

liz@trendingtoday.com

SOURCE: Trending Today

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Cyberlux Advances Execution on $20 Million Backlog with Next Delivery Milestones
data.description:
RESEARCH TRIANGLE PARK, NC / ACCESS Newswire / November 19, 2025 / Cyberlux Corporation , a leading provider of advanced mission-critical technology solutions, today announced key progress in fulfilling its existing $20 million order backlog, highlighted by the successful delivery of a major shipment of Datron system spares to a U.S. Government customer supporting a partner nation in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region.

Cyberlux has delivered $719,000 in Datron system spare parts to ensure sustainment and operational readiness for the partner nation's currently deployed V/UHF and HF systems. These systems continue to perform successfully in the field, supporting ongoing regional mission requirements.

A second shipment, valued at over $700,000, is already in process and will be delivered under the same program. These two deliveries represent substantial progress in fulfilling more than $10 million in MENA-region orders currently included in the Company's backlog.

"Cyberlux is back to executing on our 2025 and 2026 operational plan," said Mark Schmidt, CEO of Cyberlux Corporation. "These shipments reinforce our role as a trusted supplier to U.S. Government partners and allied nations. As we continue accelerating backlog fulfillment, we expect meaningful revenue conversion and expanding opportunities in the MENA region."

With the U.S. Government now fully operational, Cyberlux is currently awaiting a new MENA contract award for over $8,000,000 for additional secure radios for this nation partner. These expected orders reflect both the continuing demand for Cyberlux's solutions and the strong alignment with the U.S. foreign military support priorities in the region.

Cyberlux will provide additional updates as further deliveries, order awards, and milestones are achieved.

About Cyberlux Corporation

Cyberlux Corporation develops and delivers innovative defense technologies including unmanned aerial systems (UAS), autonomy software, tactical communications, and advanced mission-support solutions for U.S. and allied government agencies worldwide.

SAFE HARBOR STATEMENT

This Press Release may contain forward-looking statements that can be identified by terminology such as "believes," "expects," "potential," "plans," "suggests," "may," "should," "could," "intends," or similar expressions within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. All forward-looking statements are inherently uncertain as they are based on current expectations and assumptions concerning future events or future performance of the company. Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on these forward-looking statements, which are only predictions and speak only as of the date hereof. In evaluating such statements, prospective investors should review carefully various risks and uncertainties identified in this release and matters set in the company's OTC/SEC filings. These risks and uncertainties could cause the company's actual results to differ materially from those indicated in the forward-looking statements.

For media inquiries, please contact:

Email:ir_cybl@Cyberlux.com | Phone: (984) 363-6894

data.title: Corn basis steady-firmer at ethanol sites; soy bids flat
data.description:
Basis bids for corn firmed at some Midwest ethanol plants on Wednesday while bids elsewhere held mostly steady as Chicago Board of Trade corn and soy futures declined.

  • The corn basis improved by 2 to 3 cents a bushel at ethanol facilities in Union City, Indiana, and Annawan, Illinois, spot checks showed.

  • The U.S. Energy Information Administration said weekly production of corn-based ethanol increased in the latest week to 1.091 million barrels per day. Weekly ethanol stockpiles rose to 22.307 million barrels, inching back up from a one-year low set in mid-October.

  • Soybean basis bids were mostly flat after firming at river locations earlier this week, buoyed by demand from exporters along the Gulf Coast.

  • The U.S. Department of Agriculture confirmed sales of 330,000 metric tons of U.S. soybeans to China, a day after confirming sales to China of 792,000 tons of the oilseed.

  • China had bought at least four cargoes of U.S. soybeans on Tuesday night, including three from U.S. Gulf terminals, two China-based traders with knowledge of the deals said.

  • Chicago Board of Trade corn and soybean futures were lower in midday trading. As of 11:30 a.m. CST (1730 GMT), CBOT December corn (CZ25) was down 6-1/2 cents at $4.30-1/4 a bushel and January soybeans (SF26) were down 15-1/4 cents at $11.38-1/4 a bushel.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson On Track for Record High Close — Data Talk
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) is currently at $202.22, up $2.22 or 1.11%

  • Would be new all-time closing high (Based on available data back to Jan. 21, 1972)
  • On pace for largest percent increase since Nov. 17, 2025, when it rose 1.86%
  • Currently up nine of the past 10 days
  • Currently up eight consecutive days; up 8.39% over this period
  • Longest winning streak since Aug. 26, 2024, when it rose for nine straight trading days
  • Best eight day stretch since the eight days ending July 25, 2025, when it rose 8.46%
  • Up 7.07% month-to-date
  • Up 39.83% year-to-date; on pace for best year since 1995, when it rose 56.16%
  • Up 32.07% from 52 weeks ago (Nov. 20, 2024), when it closed at $153.11
  • Would be a new 52-week closing high
  • Up 42.35% from its 52-week closing low of $142.06 on Jan. 10, 2025
  • Traded as high as $202.31; new all-time intraday high (Based on available data back to Jan. 21, 1972)
  • Up 1.16% at today's intraday high
  • Second best performer in the DJIA today
  • Contributed 13.67 points to the DJIA so far today

All data as of 12:40:00 PM ET

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet

data.title: The Manufacturing & Automation eXchange Expands Its Reach with the Addition of Paint & Coatings Industry Magazine as Sixth Media Partner
data.description:
New partnership adds coatings and finishing insight to MAX 2026, expanding innovation across manufacturing sectors.

BIRMINGHAM, MI / ACCESS Newswire / November 19, 2025 / BIRMINGHAM, MI / ACCESS Newswire / November 19, 2025 / The Manufacturing & Automation eXchange (MAX), a premier event bringing together manufacturing leaders, innovators, and solution providers, is expanding its scope with the addition of Paint & Coatings Industry (PCI) magazine as its newest media partner. PCI joins five leading trade publications producing the inaugural event in Nashville, taking place March 25-26, 2026, with an additional day of educational workshops on March 24. The addition of PCI enhances MAX's ability to connect attendees with the latest advancements across the entire manufacturing ecosystem.

https://app.accessnewswire.com/imagelibrary/79a1eacc-da2c-4009-b24a-a033c8872c69/image.jpeg

With PCI's involvement, the event will now feature expanded opportunities for manufacturers to explore cutting-edge technologies and solutions in coatings, finishing, and surface treatment - critical elements in modern production and automation environments.

"We're thrilled to welcome Paint & Coatings Industry as a MAX partner," said Bill DeYoe, Group Publisher of the Manufacturing Division at BNP Media. "Their expertise in coatings and finishing brings a valuable new dimension to our event, allowing attendees to discover solutions that complete the full manufacturing process."

The addition of PCI reflects MAX's commitment to fostering cross-sector collaboration and innovation. By uniting publications that serve diverse segments of manufacturing, MAX delivers a comprehensive experience for executives seeking to improve efficiency, quality, and sustainability in their operations.

The upcoming Manufacturing & Automation eXchange will take place March 25-26, 2026 at the Music City Center in Nashville, Tennessee, offering a unique environment where manufacturing decision-makers engage directly with technology and solution providers through high-value demonstrations, meetings and networking sessions.

Attendees can register now at theMAXevent.com. Companies that want to join MAX as exhibitors or sponsors can reach the team through the exhibitor inquiry form on the site.

About the Manufacturing & Automation eXchange

The Manufacturing & Automation eXchange (MAX) is an exclusive event connecting manufacturing executives with solution providers in automation, digital transformation, robotics, packaging, adhesives, quality, and now coatings and surface technologies. Produced by leading industry publications, MAX fosters collaboration and innovation to help manufacturers address today's challenges and opportunities.

About Paint & Coatings Industry

Paint & Coatings Industry (PCI) is the leading magazine serving professionals in the global coatings industry. PCI provides news, research, and insights into coatings formulation, production, application, and testing, supporting innovation throughout the coatings supply chain.

For Further Information, Contact:

Amy Riemer, Media Relations Representative

978-502-4895 (cell)

amy@riemercommunications.com

SOURCE: Manufacturing & Automation eXchange

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire
